resources.string: When enabled, the rule only applies when both number and content match.
The delay before the call is hung up (in seconds).

Allow calls from previously dialed numbers.

Options:

- Time Range
  - All Time
  Always allow dialed numbers.
  - Within Days
  Calls that occurred before X days are ignored.

- Include SMS
When enabled, texting that number also counts.

Priority: 10

Any call would pass if any app in the list has been used recently.

Use case:
  A PizzaApp order triggers an immediate refund call due to the restaurant closing, this call passes if PizzaApp is enabled here.

The default setting 5 Min means all calls pass if any of the apps has been used within 5 minutes, by \"used\" it means any of: activated, deactivated, minimized, or killed.

Applies only to calls, not to text messages.

Priority: 10

- <b>Reject</b>:
Same as pressing the decline button. It works completely silently, lockscreen won\'t be turned on.

- <b>Silence</b>:
It will ring silently in the background, giving the impression to the caller that you were unavailable and missed the call.

It may also help if you are experiencing repeated spam calls, it will extend the call duration, making it less efficient for spammers, potentially discouraging them from calling you again.

- <b>Hang Up</b>:
Answer and hang up the call to prevent it from going to voicemail.

When the spam call is received during an ongoing call, it will switch to \"Reject\" to avoid hanging up the ongoing call.

Note The lockscreen can be turned on for a short while, you might also hear a call end tone(some phones offer an option to mute this sound).

- The delay:
  By default, there is a 1-second delay before the call is hung up, to prevent calls from accidentally going to voicemail due to rapid disconnection.
  If you want to record calls, install a call recording app and increase the delay accordingly.
